one_line: Mauryan emperor who turned from brutal conquest at Kalinga to rule by dhamma.

appearance.age_look: Mid-30s, tall and broad-shouldered, with a sturdy warrior’s build softened by a calm bearing.
appearance.face: Oval face, medium-brown North Indian complexion, straight nose, defined jaw, short neatly trimmed beard and moustache, thoughtful almond-shaped eyes with a steady gaze; default expression is composed and serious, hinting at inner remorse and resolve.
appearance.hair: Black hair gathered into a tight topknot at the crown, sides clean and orderly, no loose strands; simple gold circlet resting low on the forehead with a single rounded central ornament but no extravagant spikes or high crown.
appearance.costume: Sleeveless Mauryan-style royal tunic in muted deep red cotton reaching mid-thigh, belted at the waist with a plain broad brown leather belt and a simple rectangular bronze buckle, worn over calf-length off-white dhoti folded and pleated around the legs; soft brown leather sandals with thong between toes and ankle strap; a pale saffron cotton uttariya (shawl) draped from left shoulder across chest and hanging behind, hinting at his commitment to dhamma; minimal jewellery: a pair of plain gold hoop earrings, one simple gold armlet on the right upper arm, and a slender gold bracelet on the left wrist; no heavy ornaments or lavish embroidery, just subtle stitched borders in darker red along tunic hem and dhoti edge.
appearance.signature: A long, slightly curved iron sword with a plain wooden hilt and no ornament, sheathed in a brown leather scabbard hanging from his belt on the left side, often resting under his hand to symbolize both his past violence and restrained power.

ref_desc: Emperor Ashoka appears as a mid-30s broad-shouldered Mauryan ruler with an oval bearded face, black topknot and simple gold circlet, deep red tunic, off-white dhoti, pale saffron shawl, minimal gold jewellery, brown sandals, and a plain curved sword at his left hip.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Emperor Ashoka as a historically grounded Mauryan ruler: mid-30s, tall and sturdy, medium-brown North Indian complexion, oval face with straight nose, defined jaw, and short neatly trimmed beard and moustache, his expression composed and serious with thoughtful almond-shaped eyes. His black hair is tied into a tight topknot at the crown, framed by a simple gold circlet resting low on the forehead with a single rounded central ornament. He wears a sleeveless deep red cotton tunic reaching mid-thigh, with subtle darker red stitched borders, belted at the waist by a plain broad brown leather belt and small bronze buckle, over a calf-length off-white dhoti wrapped and pleated authentically around the legs. A pale saffron cotton shawl (uttariya) is draped from his left shoulder across his chest and hanging behind him, evoking his turn toward dhamma, and he has soft brown leather sandals with an ankle strap. Jewellery is minimal and dignified: plain gold hoop earrings, a simple gold armlet on the right upper arm, and a slender gold bracelet on the left wrist, with no heavy regalia. At his left hip hangs his signature weapon, a long slightly curved iron sword with a plain wooden hilt in a brown leather scabbard, one hand often resting on it to show restrained authority. Use deep red, off-white, pale saffron, brown, and gold as the dominant colours, and present him front-facing with clear views of face, hair, costume details, footwear, and sword for consistent redrawing.
